What's going everyone? This is going to be a dense packed presentation. So normally I talk about the sales flow decision matrix, just kind of the decision making tree to know and self diagnose what any issues in a sales flow pipeline is or are, excuse me. But I realized after having done this a lot of times that before you can diagnose problems you need to understand what the benchmarks are. So someone might say like my sales are low and then once we dig into the numbers it's like your sales are great and it's just a lot of just like expectations of what is what is normal, what is good, what is great, what is elite, etc. So I bring you benchmarks. So leads per day during the launch program for me is like this is what I would be looking for for my office. Like I would try and get to 20 leads a day for under $20 per lead. So whatever that cost is if you're getting $10 leads then it's $200 a day. If it's if you're getting $5 leads then you're only spending $50 a day. If you're getting $20 leads then you're spending $400 a day, right? So my goal is to get to 20 leads a day. In my earlier training I had 30 a day but it seems like for a lot of gyms that's too many for them to handle efficiently. So their conversion percentages drop. My guys were obviously super, super trained sales guys and all they did was sell. They weren't managing a gym, etc. So if you are going above 20 a day I might well if you're going above 20 a day be mindful of the fact that wasted leads are not good. Like it's not a good thing to be inefficient with your lead flow. I would rather you be efficient with your lead flow and close 20% of your leads by working the process rather than get buckets of leads and close 8%. So that I just want to establish that as a benchmark for leads per day and cars per lead overall. Scheduling, I'm giving you a continuum here. Average is 60%. If you're below 60% in scheduling it means you have some sort of issue and I'll dig into how to fix each one of these things but average is 60%, good is 70%, great is 80% and then a lead is 90% plus. Show rates similar 50% show rate is average, 60% is good, 70% is great, 80% is elite which is fantastic. So anything below 50% for example would be something like okay we need to fix this. So anything in the 50% plus range you can just kind of grade yourself on how good you're doing but average is the benchmark. So for me if I'm looking at a pipeline and I were like diagnosing your sales process or your funnel I would be if you're above the average then I'm good and I'll move on to the next piece of the flow. So I want to be clear here you're not shooting like yes you should shoot for 80% show rate before me if I'm diagnosing you're above 50% I'm moving on to the next piece to see if there's an issue. Close rate 50% is kind of the baseline if you're closing below 50% you're having selling issues and then we would go through kind of the selling diagnosis of what the issue is within the sale but closing you know 50% 60% is good 70% is great 80% is elite. I mean truly elite if you're closing 80% hard real closes paid your elite you're very very good. Back out and hard decline so this is after the sale benchmarks 0% is elite less than 5% is great less than 10% is good less than 15% is average anything above 15% means that there's some sort of issue where people are getting pressure closed but not the wrong like the wrong way so it's sometimes it's like selling hot which is like really not addressing it's there's an issue with the sale essentially if 15% plus are closing and we're definitely not reinforcing the sale it's like you're hurrying people out the door rather than really making sure that they feel good about the decision refund so if you after so the back out is after the sale before they start refund is more of a fulfillment thing but it's worth mentioning in benchmarks that if you refund 0 to 2.5% by the end of the program meaning either from a money back guarantee standpoint and or winning the challenge standpoint they don't want to continue and they want to get their money back then 0 to 2.5% is elite 2.5% to 5% is great 5 to 7.5 is good 7.5 to 10 is average if you're over 10% on your refund then you need to look at your fulfillment and that there might be issues mind you underneath I always say plan for 20% refund just in terms of like I just never want anyone I never want anyone to be caught you know with their pants down I haven't seen I mean basically the money that you are getting here like you just need to always make sure that you can fulfill on your promises 20% more than covers that in virtually all the scenarios that we have seen yeah so just to be clear 20% like you should never even get close to that and that's that's kind of the point so was it pray for sunshine or yeah pray for sunshine prepare for rain like that's that's kind of the thought process here if you're like I'm gonna have to give back 20% I sure hope not that's not the point the point is to plan for that always meant for worst case scenario but these are these are the averages of what what we see and that concludes our benchmark section so that should give you an idea of where you're at and where the issue might be alright so when we're assessing your your your pipeline this should give you an idea of what the problem is and then once we have the problem once we've identified what the problem is by percentage then we can move forward with the diagnostic kind of fixes
